emotional_core: The invisible emotional weight we carry in silence costs us more than the visible burdens we talk about — and acknowledgment, not resolution, is the first way out.
visual_metaphor: a person sitting in a well-lit room that looks normal from outside, carrying something that has no shape — presence without peace, function without ease
key_quotes: Silence has a texture people can feel.
The carrying itself becomes the familiar thing. Even the weight feels like home.
You are not as good at hiding it as you think. And even if you were — you deserve to put it down.

story_frames.visual: Person sitting alone near a window, soft natural light, looking out — not sad, just distant. No phone, no book. Still inside a quiet that has weight.
story_frames.text: There's a version of you most people won't meet.

story_frames.visual: Close-up of hands at a keyboard, slightly blurred as if attention is elsewhere. Screen glow, dim room. The work is happening but something else is too.
Two people at a cafe table, both looking in different directions. Not fighting. Just somewhere else. The gap between them has no name.
story_frames.text: Silence has a texture people can feel.

story_frames.visual: A person carefully packing something into a bag they never open. Deliberate. Quiet. Like they've done it before and will again.
story_frames.text: Putting it down means looking at it first.

story_frames.visual: Overhead shot of a journal open to a blank page, single pen beside it, early morning light. The page is still empty but the intention to write is there.
story_frames.text: Name it. Even only to yourself.

story_frames.visual: Person standing outdoors at golden hour, hand flat on chest, eyes closed — not dramatic, just still. A moment of contact with something interior.
story_frames.text: You don't have to resolve it today.

story_frames.visual: Same window as frame 1, light shifted slightly warmer. Person still present, but posture fractionally more open. No dramatic change. Just a degree of ease.
